methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $700M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $1.0B - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2.96B

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Amogy, a Series C climate and energy company founded by Seonghoon Woo, is valued at $700M post-money after raising $300M+. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private frontier-tech firms (including KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, Redwood Materials, and QuantumScape) and 1 public comparable (Linamar, LNZA), all operating in climate and energy infrastructure.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier to the $700M last post-money, implying $1.2B. Peer-median analysis across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Redwood Materials at $6.0B (Series D), KoBold Metals at $3.0B (Series C), and Electric Hydrogen at $1.0B (Series C)—yields a median of $3.0B, with a range of $1.0B to $6.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital requirements across the climate-tech landscape.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $1.0B (low) to $3.4B (high), with a median of $2.1B. The current $700M valuation sits below this range, suggesting meaningful upside potential under normal Series C progression. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within the peer set, path to commercialization, and capital efficiency relative to comparables.
